entry.title: How do i create GL accounts code for a list of prepaid cards ( Assets )

SAP S4/HANA CLOUD FIORI

How to you create GL Accounts code for prepaid cards (debit card) - Assets?

What about also credit cards?

Do you treat it the same as banks?

entry.title: input tax distribution and correction according to § 15 of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

this is about the input tax distribution according to § 15 of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG) and the input tax correction according to § 15a of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG) in the SAP S4HANA Private Cloud.

 

Input tax distribution according to § 15 UStG or § 15 (4) UStG:

Input tax distribution means dividing the input tax amounts into two parts, one part, the non-deductible input tax amount, and the other part, the deductible input tax amount.

The amounts of the input tax which are deductible and not, is determined by their allocation to the revenues. If the input tax is allocated to the revenues subject to VAT, the input tax is deductible; if the input tax is allocated to the revenues exempt from VAT, the input tax is not deductible.

 

For this allocation, an allocation key is calculated as follows: VAT-exempt revenues are divided by VAT-liable revenues, resulting in the allocation key.

The input tax amounts are then divided into deductible input tax and non-deductible input tax using this allocation key, and posted in the system.

 

Input tax correction according to § 15a of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG):

At the end of the fiscal year, after all revenues have been posted, the allocation key is recalculated and updated.

The input tax correction is executed using the updated key. All posted input tax amounts which were calculated using the old allocation key, are corrected by using the new allocation key.

 

This needs to be done in the accounts receivable ledger, the general ledger, and for assets in the fixed asset ledger.

 

What options does SAP S4HANA Private Cloud offer for the accounts receivable subledger, the general ledger, and the fixed asset subledger?

Is there a way to use a Tax on Sales/Purchases code for this, which can then be updated and which corrects the input tax amounts automatically?

 

I would be very thankful for any answer or idea, would be highly appreciated. 🙂

 

Best regards,

A. Regensburger

entry.title: Re: Deletion of CI_COBL
entry.content:
So you didn't read 3456968 - Deletion of coding block fields from CI_COBL ...

'The only recommendation is to customize the field as suppressed in all field status groups (in transaction OB14 and OB41) to hide the field from entry or display in any standard transaction, but you should NEVER delete the field from CI_COBL structure or any database table it was included.'

entry.title: Inserted actions do not work anymore
entry.content:
Hello 

We are facing an issue with recordings in Sap Enable Now since this week. 

When you look at a demo, the clicks that are added after generating the simulation are in the left upper corner of the screen and not in the place we put them. It seems to be that it's only happening to the manually added clicks and not in the ones that are generated in the process of the recording. But before, it worked just fine for the added actions/clicks. 

Is this a known issue? Can we do something about it? 

Kind regards 

Laura

entry.title: ABAP DUMP 'SAPLV13A' DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR. How to handle this DUMP in SAP
entry.content: This happens when condition for which we are loading this per 0 scale value. The purpose is so that when the quantity of the material group on the order reaches 5000 plus they no longer pay the freight surcharge rate.

entry.title: Absence Time Type Writes 0 Hours on Holidays
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm trying to create a custom absence time type (e.g., Holiday Substitute) in SAP SuccessFactors Time Off, intended only for reporting purposes — specifically, to record 8 hours on public holidays and non-working days even though the day has no scheduled hours (holidays show 0 in Planned Working Time).
My goals are to add a time type to appear in Story Reports and Time Sheet UI and that time type must write a fixed 8-hour duration even on non-working days or holidays (No payroll integration involved – this is purely for reporting.

The method I used: 

1. Absence Counting Configuration with seemingly correct configuration and business rule assigned:  
IF <conditions> (always true)
THEN Absence Counting Rule Result Parameters.Deduction Quantity = 8

2. Defined Time Type Collision Grouping - The collision does resolve correctly when overlapping absences are created — the new time type replaces the previous one.

Result is in the Time Sheet, when this time type is used on a holiday, it shows up but with 0 hours and the same duration is written in Recorded time data v2 query table. 

I also tried creating time valuations with valuation type:  1. calculation by business rule and 2. create time type but these require Time Tracking to be enabled, which is not available in our license.

Is there a way to bypass the 0-hour issue when using only Time Off (no Time Tracking)? Is my rule maybe the issue?
Any workarounds, tips, or examples of similar setups would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance!

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Re: Accessing MIGO Through Fiori Launchpad on Mobile Device
entry.content:
Now with access with our development/customizing client, I was able to complete the following:

- Created an adaptive flavor built for a tablet using the "Post Goods Movement" app (classic MIGO)
- Created an assignment category and related that flavor to that assignment category.
- Checked that my user has role for BR_FLAVOR_CONSUMER which contains the catalogue entry for "User Interface - Personas Usage Display". I have also included a restriction on this business role for the assignment category I previously created.
- Finally, I created a custom tile with the given URL and assigned to a catalogue in the fiori launchpad.

my######.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui#Material-postGoodsMovementInWebGUI?sap-ui-tech-hint=GUI&sap-personas-flavor=35A385630C061FE084AB5609C5BA0538

I can now see my custom tile, and when I click on the tile on my desktop the flavor is launched directly.

However, I am still running into an issue with the tablet/smartphone. I can see the custom tile when using my mobile device, but when I try to access it I keep getting this error message:

"app could not be opened either due to an incorrect configuration or a missing role assignment.

The navigation target "#Material-postGoodsMovementInWebGUI?sap-personas-flavor=8B9DFF41306A1E082CE7208168AC534&sap-ui-tech-hint=GUI" of the app could not be resolved. This can be caused, for example, by an incorrect SAP Fiori launchpad content configuration or an incorrect or missing role assignment,"

I'm really not sure what else I am missing...

@Tamas_Hoznek

entry.title: Re: Purchasing organization for direct and indirect procu...
entry.content:
Akash 

Here are a few pointers that may help you think logically and have a apporach.

Creating two purchasing orgs means you have signing up more data maintenance -- I would recommend that you should keep one purchasing org and use other key data elements to identify indirect procurement.

1. I will keep one purchasing org but purchasing groups --you can for Direct -- they will have DXX and indirect they will have IXX -- 

2. Your material groups -- use for Service and one use materials keep them separate --examples --Service material groups start with S and materials groups start with M

3.  For reporting point of view..Indirect materials procurement always has account assignment and direct materials will come to inventory then consumption happen 

4. Other option--PR and PO document types -- one for Indirect procurement and one for direct procurement --that way you clear distinction without increasing work on master data maintenance.

entry.title: Re: Cannot receive Order Confirmation Request linked from...
entry.content: Are you maintaining a confirmation control key on the purchase order line item ? under the confirmation tab? if yes please share the screenshot

entry.title: Re: Balance in transaction currency is too large for auto...
entry.content:
After analysis and tests to discover what could be happening, I realized in an example scenario that both documents were being passed to this clearing API: the invoice, a document of type RE, and the vendor payment, type KZ.

For this API, both documents must be passed together, one as a credit and the other as a debit, where one will be positive and the other negative. As expected, the sum of the lines must be equal to 0, otherwise you will receive this balance error.

For my scenario, I implemented the Journal Entry BOI, posting a document of type KZ and then called the clearing API. Just one point of attention: it's the item that will be passed for clearing. In my case, I had to pass item 2, because item 1 was returning a message that was already cleared item.

Thank you for your help, Jeremy, and I hope this has helped you all.

Best Regards,

Marcelo R.

entry.title: BTE or substitution does not work druing FB70 simulation
entry.content:
Problem:
In FB70 we use tax, which automatically adds a new row to the document. However this row does not have FIPOS, GSBER and GEBER fields filled. I  wanto to copy them from the other lines, and possibly display them to the user during simulation.

Tried solution:

BTE 1120 - it works after the save button.
GGB1 - defined user exit (c_exit_param_class) must work on callup point 3, to be able to copy fileds from other line items. Thus works after Save, but not on simulation.

Please provide an idea.

P.S. we have an error F5A049 on that line, which is not caused by OB14, OB41 or any substituion/validation.

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content:
First of all look at server properties:

NumLogicalProcessors, NumLogicalProcessorsUsed, NumPhisicalProcessors, NumPhisicalProcessorsUsed

So you are sure how many phisical cpu SQL Anywhere use

Then it depends on query complexity; if you use database parameter maxquerytask = 0 you can benefit of intraquery parallelism

entry.title: Where can I learn to configure SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay?
entry.content:
recommend to access our Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the configurations necessary to implement SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to perform and configure the core functionalities within SAP SuccessFactors Variable Pay and access the resources and support available to configure additional features.

 

Prerequisites

  •  Exploring SAP SuccessFactors Platform

Prepare for certification

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Operationalizing value realization by enriching data in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Adding non-system context by enriching data in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to support operationalizing value realization

Have you ever thought about enriching your data in process analysis with additional non-system context?

No?

Why not?

Context is king – and it might help you to realize potential value identified through SAP Signavio Process Intelligence!

For example, when wanting to perform additional root cause analysis on information that is not directly available as part of the data set, but SME input is required. Or, in very specific cases, e.g. for value realization tracking on improvement initiatives & task backlog management.

Today, we will look into a very specific example where this might be relevant (besides many other examples where you might want to add e.g. root cause information to cases):

Value realization tracking for duplicate invoices

The value accelerator „Reduce Duplicate Invoices for Invoice to Pay” for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence delivers you sophisticated analysis to identify potential invoice duplicates. It is available for general usage in the Value Accelerator Library.

The word potential is important here. It is now up to your own company's experts to qualify and validate these results, afterwards. In this process, you don’t want to look at the same invoices again and again – and you also want to see the impact of the analysis on your company’s finances.

This is where the approach we want to introduce you today gets handy.

But how do you know that you have already identified an invoice as a duplicate or already touched it? How do you know, how much money you have already saved your company by using SAP Signavio Process Intelligence?

Let’s imagine you have already set up the duplicate invoice checker, have identified value potential and now want to work on realizing value.

Example intro image for the duplicate invoice checker:

By extracting data from Signavio Process Intelligence via ODATA API (e.g. into SAP Build or into Excel, based on this blog post), changing data in e.g. Excel and sending the data back into the SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Ingestion API, it is possible to enrich your process mining data with additional context (e.g. “is this really a duplicate invoice?” or add root cause information).

Let’s imagine, we have already declared six invoices as duplicates and deferred one as no duplicate: 

A first dashboard that identified the confirmed six duplicates could then look like this:

This allows us to a) integrate the context (e.g. is this invoice a real duplicate?) directly back into dashboards and ultimately b) measure value realization in this case.

A second data load now shows additional invoices identified (seven more duplicates):

The last step is the setup of the value realization tracking.

To track progress on value realization, it is recommended to set up a metric. In this case we have created two metrics and track the progression of your value case. In this example, we have set up two metrics: “Duplicate invoices identified” and “Duplicate payments mitigated (value realized)”.

Throughout the data loads when updating data, you can now monitor the value realization progression per day, week, month, quarter or year: 

Et voila, you have unlocked additional potential of your data!

Any help needed?

We're here to support you on your journey to value realization and help you maximize outcome. Also with custom solutions. When it comes to identifying and delivering true business value, SAP Signavio Professional Services is your trusted partner.

Feel free to reach out to your customer success partner for further information!

And now: Happy value realization!

Info: The approach introduced is a low effort approach requiring no additional licenses. It might not be suitable for every enterprise. We are also happy to support in setting up more sophisticated enterprise-ready solutions for this approach together with you (e.g. including SAP Build).

PS: You can of course also use this approach to enrich your cases with root cause information (e.g. “Why was this invoice a duplicate?”).

entry.title: Re: IBP Automated outlier correction

Could you also share a screenshot of the operator configuration?

entry.title: In S4HC, report showing Sales Order Line Items
entry.content:
The Manage Sales Order app in S4 Public Cloud shows sales orders at the order level.  That's nice but not what we need.  Is a similar app available at the LINE ITEM level?  We need to see what products were sold, who they were sold to, where they shipped, and the unit price that we charged.  Is this report available in S4 Public Cloud?

The List Sales Orders app includes a tab called Sales Order Items.  It is almost what we need.  The problem is that it lacks unit sales price.  It includes a Net Value field, which is the total price for the line item even if it's multiple units, and it includes freight.  But again we need to see the unit selling price, for one unit, without freight or anything else included.  So SAP if you're reading, please consider adding unit selling price to this report.

Thanks!

Ralph

3038789 - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR occurs when creating new condition record

entry.title: Price change in Auto Renewal in Subscription Order management

We have a business scenario to extend the contract(Auto Renewal) with price increments each year.

As per the "Auto Renewal" features for SOM, it doesn't mentioned if it is allowed to increase the price for each renewal.

Please advise if the "Auto Renewal" features supports price increment.

 

Thanks in advance

Anil

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs

entry.title: Re: #CoE #DH01 how to know which Fiori app is used by whi...
entry.content:
Hi yuki_0717

I agree it can be a bit confusing.

It helps to understand the different between Fiori apps and the legacy, Harmonized GUI apps.

The Harmonized GUI apps have a tcode (transaction) whereas the Fiori apps are services based.

So for the Harmonized GUI you would see the Transaction listed in the Event Message Text, and are associated with the Event ID AU3 (Transaction &A started).

For Fiori, you will see more the security authorization check for the underlying service, and are associated with GU1 (Start Authority Check for &A (&B) successful).

two examples:

Create Sales Order VA01 will show up with AU3, Event text is Transaction VA01 Started.

The View Browser will show up with GU1, Event text is Start authority check for View Browser Service ( CDSALLVIEWS ) successful. (and ABAP Source is CDSALLVIEWS)

Indeed, in the Fiori App Library for View Browser we see the service is CDSALLVIEWS

It would be nice if they could add the AppId to the output of the Audit Log Events.

If you would consider to create an Influence Request, and share it among your peers, that will help to get it improved!

Hopefully this helps

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: SAP CI IS Signed Packages and Extension Flows - Billed?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm having a healthy debate with my line about billing for messages in SAP IS CI.  Here's the scenario:

We have an ancient implementation of a C4C <--> ERP package.  We'd like to bring it up to date, and make it entirely standard so that our SAP to SAP messages will be free.  Thing is, we modify our outbound documents for the inevitable business reasons.

That's obviously where Extension Flows come in - and where the question happens.  The message from C4C <--> ERP would be free... but what about the process direct call to the extension flow?  Those flows won't send or receive data - they just make modifications to the messages for our various business reasons.  Would they, therefore, also be free as then do not cross the external boundary of the tenant?

Tl;dr  Do self contained process direct flows called from signed package incur a cost?

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content:
Please check the following query:

select  property('MinMultiprogrammingLevel'),
           property('CurrentMultiprogrammingLevel'),
           property('MaxMultiprogrammingLevel')

You will likely need to up the maximum multiprogramming level (-gnh).  Unless you have set this already, it is likely too small  given the number of logical processors.  I suspect that the max is currently 80, in the absence of that switch? Sounds like you have beefy hardware so I would consider upping the max to 500 perhaps? Running with -gns  to monitor the engines adjustments to the MPL  is also suggested.    It will output lines like:

          MPL[curr=20 prev=20] concurrency[0] throughput[curr=74 prev=79] algorithm=2 

If the maximum MPL curr reported  in these statistics is near the MaxMultiprogrammingLevel, it may suggest that the MaxMultiprogrammingLevel should be increased.  Note that there is a balancing act here as the MPL configuration impacts memory usage.

Please your help.

Thanks

Diego Schlimovich

entry.title: Re: How to use Smart Link in Fiori Elements
entry.content: Bumping

entry.title: Bypass Biographical Info RBP check for Reporting of Person-Related Data in People Analytics
entry.content:
Many customers are hesitating to provide the permission for biographical information to all report users. The reason is that biographical information has sensitive fields like “Date of Birth”, “Birth Name”, “Region of Birth” but does not provide field-level permissions. If the permission for biographical information is not provided, then up to now it was not possible to report on additional person related details (such as e.g. address, contact details like phone number and email, work permit details or dependents). Starting from H1 2505 a long-awaited possibility to bypass biographical information in story reporting is available which still allows to report on additional person related details.

Introduction

The role-based permissions for (1) the “Effective Dated Employee Central Entities” like “Personal Information”, “Address Information” and “Dependents” and (2) the “Employee Data” like “Biographical Information”, “Phone- and Email Information”, “Social Accounts”, “Work Eligibility”, “National ID” and “Emergency Contact” are provided independently. As consequence the individual Employee Profile portlets will show / hide the respective details. See the example shown at Figure 1, the “Biographical Information” details shown in red and the details “Work Eligibility”, “Contact Information” and “Emergency Contact” shown in yellow.

Figure 1   Example of an Employee Profile which shows details from Biographical information, Work Eligibility, Contact information and the Emergency Contact

If the permission for “Biographical Information” is not granted as indicated in Figure 2, the “Biographical Information” portlet will not appear at the employee profile UI, while the other portlets are still visible. However, reporting worked differently, see Figure 3 for the query design: Without permission for “Biographical Information” the “Person ID” (indicated in red) remained empty and no person-related data (4 objects indicated in yellow) are accessible which are joined via “Biographical Information”.

Figure 2   RBP setting under “Employee Data” -> “HR Information” for the “Biographical Information”

Figure 3   Query design to report on person-related details

Solution Approach

Starting from H1 2505 a solution was implemented to bypass “Biographical Information” in story reporting. If the permission for “Biographical Information” is not granted, then all fields appear with a locker symbol except the field “Person ID” and a few other rather technical fields like “Created/Last Modified By”, “Creation/Last Modified Date” (see Figure 4). Note that the numeric “Person ID” should not by exchanged with the “Person ID External” (which is on the employee UI often just named as “Person ID”).

Figure 4   Query design to report on person-related details

Figure 5 shows the result of such a query. Fields which remained previously empty like the details from “Phone Number”, “Email”, “Work Permit” and “Emergency Contact” are now filled (while “Person ID External” remains empty as expected).

Figure 5   Query result which shows details from person-related objects which were previously hidden

entry.title: Usage of Currency Conversion Functionality in People Analytics Story Reporting
entry.content:
Starting from H1 2505 the long-awaited and urgently needed functionality of currency conversion in story reporting is available. Before it is described how to use the currency conversion object in queries and story reports, it is described how the currency conversion functionality works in Success Factors and which data manipulations are required to make the object functional for reporting.

Introduction

There are multiple use cases for the usage of currency conversions, such as e.g.

  • Payments for Base Salary are paid in Euros and Bonus Payments are paid in US dollars. For the calculation of the monthly or annual salary it is required to convert all payments into a single currency. This target currency could be the “country currency”, the “Legal Entity currency” or the “Pay Component Group currency”.
  • Even if all pay components are paid in a single currency (e.g. EUR) it could be possible that the pay ranges are defined in another currency (e.g. USD). For the calculation of the compa ratio or the range penetration, it is required to use the currency conversion.
  • Another reporting use case is to convert all compensation amounts into one target currency that is freely entered by a report user.

The currency exchange rate is stored in an MDF object “Currency Exchange Rate”. There are a couple of aspects to consider that will become relevant for reporting:

  1. “Currency Exchange Rate” is effective-dated.
  2. There is a “Type of Currency Exchange Rate” which allows to maintain different rates for different purposes. There is a picklist for this field. However, note that “Default” is used for the “Type of Currency Exchange Rate” in the Success Factors applications (there is currently no configuration possibility to use values different from “Default” in Success Factors).
  3. The system allows to maintain the conversion rate in both directions; however, this is not a must.
    See the example from Figure 1: In addition to the record shown in Figure 1 it is possible to maintain a record for DEFAULT-EUR-USD with any “Exchange Rate”.
    IMPORTANT: Even if no opposite direction record is maintained, the system automatically calculates and uses the conversion with the inverted rate. For the example of Figure 1 it means, that a conversion from EUR to USD with an exchange rate of 1.0487 (=1/0.9536) is used.
  4. Note that it is not possible to maintain entries where the source currency is identical to the target currency. If it is tried to post that, the error message “Exchange rate with the same source currency and target currency is not allowed” prevents saving of such records. This will become relevant later in this blogpost when the usage of the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object in reporting is discussed.

Figure 1   MDF object Currency Exchange Rate

With regard to reporting this brings some significant challenges, because simple enablement of the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object for reporting is not sufficient. In the following these challenges and the respective enhancements are explained, before some practical examples are presented how the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object with the enhanced records can be used in reporting.

Employee Profile Behaviour and Reporting Challenge

To illustrate the previously mentioned “challenges”, let’s have a look into a concrete example:

(1) See in Figure 2 the pay components in the employee profile of employee “Siva Pragadeeshadmin”: There are 3 pay components, (a) “Pay Component 3” with 777 USD (Frequency: Annual), (b) “Basic” with 4000 INR (Frequency: Half-Yearly) and (c) “Base_Salary” with 1000 EUR (Frequency: Monthly). The goal is to convert all amounts into a target currency “US Dollar” (e.g. because the Pay Component Group Sum for “PCG1_su” is configured in USD as indicated in Figure 2 in red).

Figure 2   Multiple pay components with different currencies used for employee “Siva Pragadeeshadmin”

(2) Let’s have a look what this means for the currency conversion:

  1. No conversion is required for “Pay Component 3” since the amount of 777 USD is already in the requested target currency.
  2. A record in the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object is maintained for the currency conversion of Indian rupees (source currency INR) to US dollars (target currency USD) with rate 0.001191
  3. See Figure 1 above, a record in the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object is maintained for the currency conversion of USD (source currency) to EUR (target currency) with rate 0.9536. However, no conversion rate is maintained for EUR to USD

(3) See Figure 3 for a simple query which connects the original amount-field (compensation-amount) with the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object. The goal would be to convert the original amounts into a “converted amount” as “compensation-amount” multiplied with the “conversion rate”. If just the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object is joined without a filter for the target currency, then you get many records: Let us assume that there are 20 conversion rates each of EUR, INR and USD into different target currencies, then the query described in Figure 3 results in 60 (=3 x 20) records for employee “Siva Pragadeeshadmin”. If now a filter is applied for target currency = USD, then only one records remains, i.e. the record for  “Basic” with amount 4000 and source currency INR and target currency USD. Reason: There is no record for source currency = target currency = USD to show the record for pay component “Pay Component 3” and there is no record for source currency = EUR and target currency = USD to show the record for pay component “Base_Salary”.

Figure 3   Illustration how to join the “Currency Exchange Rate” object

Solution Approach

The “trick” which is used in this context is to create additional records in the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object. It is important to emphasize that this creation of additional records happens during report runtime; nothing is persisted on the data base. To differentiate the persisted records and the ‘artificially’ added records a new column “TYPE” was introduced (don't exchange this column TYPE with the field "Type of Currency Exchange Rate"; both fields have nothing to do with each other). The logic is the following:

  1. The persisted records get a label “Standard” within the column “TYPE”
  2. For each currency which is maintained in the instance, a record with source currency = target currency and a rate = “1” is created which get a label “Auto” in the column “TYPE”
  3. If no opposite direction record is maintained in the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object, the system automatically creates an additional record with the inverted rate and a label “Reverse” in the column "TYPE". For the example of Figure 1 it means, that a record with “Source Currency” EUR and “Target Currency” USD is generated with an exchange rate of 1.0487 (=1/0.9536) and TYPE “Reverse”.

Figure 4 shows how the final output looks (the records relevant for the example are shown in red).

Figure 4   Illustration of the currency exchange rate records for the 3 different TYPES “Standard”, “Auto” and “Reverse”

Practical Examples

This “enhanced” Currency Exchange Rate-object can be used in various manners. In the following some examples are presented to give report designers an idea how this could look like:

  1. Use case is the conversion of all compensation amounts into the legal entity currency (alternatively, it is possible e.g. to use the country currency or others).
    Example: “Legal Entity Boston” has currency USD and “Legal Entity Paris” has currency EUR. Then, all compensation amounts of “Jack Miller” who is assigned to “Legal Entity Boston” are converted into USD and all compensation amounts of “Francois Bernard” who is assigned to “Legal Entity Paris” are converted into EUR.
    Figure 5 shows the respective query. The conversion happens with the help of a calculated column as shown in Figure 6. Here, a query filter is used that the target currency = legal entity currency (Figure 7). Figure 8 shows the report result for the employee “Siva Pragadeeshadmin”. You can see those records from the “currency exchange rate”-object of 3 different “TYPES” that are used in the output, (1) “Auto” for the conversion of 777 USD to 777 USD, (2) “Standard” for the conversion of 4000 INR into 47.632 USD and (3) “Reverse” for the conversion of 1000 EUR into 1048.6577 USD.

    Figure 5   Query for the usage of the currency exchange rate object (Legal Entity Currency = Target Currency)


    Figure 6   Creation of a query calculated columns for the converted amount


    Figure 7   Filter for Legal Entity Currency = Target Currency


    Figure 8   Final report output with the converted amounts

  2. A similar idea is the usage of an input parameter as target currency, i.e. the report user can freely enter any currency as target currency and all compensation amounts are converted into this target currency.
    Figure 9 shows the definition of the input parameter TargetCurrency with the default EUR, Figure 10 shows the query and Figure 11 shows the filter that is used as target currency = input parameter. Figure 12 shows the report output for employee “Siva Pragadeeshadmin” with target currency EUR. Note that one “currency exchange rate”-record of TYPE “Auto” is used while the other two records are using records of TYPE “Standard”

    Figure 9   Usage of an input parameter as target currency


    Figure 10   Query for the usage of the currency exchange rate object (Target Currency entered as input parameter)


    Figure 11   Filter for currency as input parameter = Target Currency


    Figure 12   Final report output with the converted amounts

  3. Both approaches described at (1) and (2) can also be achieved with data blending in a more elegant way (data blending will be available also in the "optimized user experience" later in 2025). If there are multiple amount fields which needs to be converted, the blending approach has advantages since it is not required to join the “Currency Exchange Rate” object multiple times but to use it only once and to do the conversion of the amounts in the story. The approach presented here used the currency of the Legal Entity to which the employee is assigned as target currency.
    To achieve that the following steps are required:
    • Creation of a simplified query as indicated in Figure 13. Furthermore, it is required to create a 2nd data source just based on the “Currency Exchange Rate” object.
    • The “Linking” of both data source is shown in Figure 14.
    • Figure 15 shows the creation of the converted amount. Note that the amount comes from the 1st data source while the rate comes from the 2nd – ‘blended’ - data source.
    • Figure 16 illustrates the creation of the final report. Note that fields of the tables come from two different data sources.

      Figure 13   Main query for the blending approach


      Figure 14   Linking of the two data sources


      Figure 15    Story calculated measure for the converted amount


      Figure 16   Report design for the usage of data blending (Legal Entity Currency = Target Currency)

  4. Also, with the “blending-approach” it is possible to use the target currency as input parameter. Following step are required to achieve this:
    • Creation of one data source just based on the “Recurring Compensation”-object (maybe plus “Job Information” -> “Personal Information” to get the employee’s name) and one data source based on the “Currency Exchange Rate” object (plus join the “Target Currency”)
    • The definition of the input parameter in the 2nd data source (see Figure 9) and the definition of a filter that the target currency is equal to the input parameter (Figure 11).
    • For the linking of both data source, just the 1st condition shown in Figure 14 is required, i.e. “Compensation Currency Code” of the 1st data source equal to the “Source Currency Code” of the 2nd data source.
    • The creation of the converted amount happens in the same way as indicated in Figure 15.

Conclusion

The presented approach gives an idea how the currency conversion functionality can be used in concrete user scenarios. Performance measurements have been conducted which indicates that the performance will be increased by roughly a factor of 5 compared to the scenario where no currency conversion is used at all. No significant differences have been observed if the target currency is used from the business data (Legal entity currency, country currency, pay component group currency) or entered as input parameter. The blending approach might be 20% slower compared to the direct usage of the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object in the source query; however, it is very likely that the blending approach will have performance advantages if the “Currency Exchange Rate”-object is multiply joined in the source query.

entry.title: LMS:How to configure Learning History connector to capture Task IDs and completion date in Checklist
entry.content:
LMS : How to configure Learning History connector to capture individual Task ID's and their completion date for Checklists

We have gone through the Learning History documentation and couldn't find any fields in it that we can use to capture Task ID in Checklist and their completion dates individually in addition to capturing the overall Checklist completion date.

We did check if there are any API"s available and received a setback for this requirement to get it done via API"s. So, we wanted to check if we can achieve this via file processing approach using Learning History connector for the learnings captured in an external tool or external learning completions.

entry.title: Re: LMS HRBP/Manager Team List
entry.content: It may be possible you are encountering the issue due to browser zoom, make sure it is set to 100% and see if the same arises.  See KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3551854

entry.title: Re: PC00_M32_CAFL0 preliminar, reporte previo.
entry.content:
Nos compartieron la siguiente nota preliminar que nos ayudó a solucionar el problema mencionado

3559941 - [MX] HMXCAFL0 - Modo preliminar no disponible al seleccionar la notificación oficial - SAP for Me

Saludos.

entry.title: Re: LMS: Is it possible to create an item with an Ongoing completion status - not for credit?
entry.content: SCORM details in particular may try to update item completion make sure you do not have that selected udner the content completion status and then mark the item to complete when all completed ensurign it has been reassigned after making changes to the item type associated with the not-for-credit completion to see if that works for you?

entry.title: Issue in Storage Location Determination in SAP ECC PP Discrete Manufacturing from Production Version
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am facing an issue related to the storage location determination in SAP PP Discrete Manufacturing when multiple production versions are maintained for a finished good (FG) material.

Scenario:

I have two active production versions for the same FG material, with different issue storage locations assigned to each:

Production Version 1: Issue Storage Location = "SLOC_1"

Production Version 2: Issue Storage Location = "SLOC_2"

In the MRP configuration, I have maintained a setting for the combination of Plant and MRP Group as "2" for storage location determination.

I have not maintained any default issue storage location in the material master or BOM component details.

Transaction & Observed Behavior:

I created a production order manually using transaction CO01. The system automatically determined Production Version 2 based on the lot size.

In the Production Order header (under the Master Data tab), I could see that Production Version 2 was selected, but in the Component Overview of the production order, the Issue Storage Location was still showing SLOC_1 instead of SLOC_2, which should be the expected behavior based on the determined production version.

If I delete Production Version 1, the system updates the issue storage location correctly to SLOC_2.

In summary, when multiple production versions are available, the system appears to default to the issue storage location of the first production version.

Analysis & Gap: This behavior seems to be a gap in the system, as the issue storage location should be updated to reflect the storage location associated with the production version that is automatically determined (in this case, SLOC_2 for Production Version 2).

Request: Could you please advise on how to address this issue so that the system correctly updates the issue storage location based on the determined production version? Any insights on how to resolve this gap would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Dinesh Kumar V

entry.title: Re: Stop very long running SAT Trace
entry.content:
Many thanks, Ankit.

By RATRA_REORG_TRACE_FILES I was able to delete a running evaluation.

entry.title: Error Messages in Tables for Time Entry or Allowances
entry.content:
Hello

Is there a table that houses Error Messages when using SFCD Tcode for loading time and allowances? We are trying to formalize some documentation and have looked in T100 but nothing related to time is noted.

Thanks

Michelle

entry.title: Re: Facing an issue with BTP CAP application deployment -...
entry.content: Has anyone figured this out? What is causing the issue and how to resolve?

entry.title: In CDS view group by clause not aggregating all records
entry.content:
Hello,

I have CDS view with Group by clause and result expecting one record but its giving three records.

I have used all fields in group by clause, The records are not aggregating as single record.

Any annotation require to aggregate or something missing in select statement?

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 1H 2025 Release of Succession Management
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2025 release is here!

The eagerly anticipated SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2025 Release is here, bringing a host of impactful features and enhancements. Let’s dive into what’s new!

1- AI-Assisted Successor Insights:

Leveraging generative AI capabilities, succession planners can now gain insights into the successors that are nominated for a position or an incumbent.

In the Succession Org Chart or the Position Tile view, users can access the feature by selecting the Generate Insights button in the header of a position card. Note that this button is available only when the position or person has at least 1 but no more than 10 successors. To produce the insights, generative AI uses the following data:
• Competencies that are associated with the position through job role
• Successors' skills and competencies
• Successors' latest overall performance ratings
• Successors' work experience that is available in their job information

In the Successor Insights dialog, the insights are organized into the following sections:

Overall Summary: Provides a high-level overview of the job role requirements and all nominated successors,
containing two parts:
• Role Overview
• Successors Analysis

Successors: Provides a summary for each successor in the following aspects:
• Experience and Progression
• Performance and Key Achievements
• Strengths and Potential Areas for Growth

Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

Configuration Requirements:

  • Purchase the AI units. For more information, please contact your Account Executive.
  • You've consented to the AI Usage Acknowledgment Statement.
  • You've selected the AI Services Administration>Assisted Successor Insights option.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrators have the Administrator>Manage AI Capabilities>AI Services Administration permission.
  • Enable User>AI Access>Assisted Successor Insights permission.

2- Approve or Decline Talent Pool Nominations on the Home Page:

On the Talent Pool Nominee home page card, users can now view the status of pending nominations, and quickly approve or decline new nominations or changes to existing nominations.

Enablement: Automatically on

Configuration Requirements:

  • To view the Talent Pool Nominee card on the home page, a user must be a talent pool owner.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Users have the User Permissions>Miscellaneous Permissions>Talent Pool>View Current permission.
  • Users have the following permissions under User Permissions>Succession Planners:View Talent Pool Nominations and Approve/Reject Talent Pool Nominations

3- Exclusion of Deleted Forms' Ratings Applicable to More Matrix Grids:

Besides the matrix grid reports in Succession, you can now exclude deleted forms' ratings from the mini matrix grids on the talent card and in the Overview block in the legacy People Profile.

To achieve this, select Exclude deleted forms when retrieving ratings in one or both of the following admin tools:

Matrix Grid Reports: Performance-Potential
Matrix Grid Reports: How vs. What

Enablement: Customer Configured

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • You have the following permissions under Administrator Permissions>Manage Succession: Performance-Potential Configuration and  How vs. What Configuration

4- Filter Position Tile View with Generic Objects:

Users can now add Generic Objects as filters to narrow down the positions displayed in the Position Tile view.

With the setting Include Generic Objects in filters in the Position Tile view enabled, if you've added Generic Object fields as searchable fields to the Position object, those Generic Objects will show up as available filters in the Position Tile view.

This enhancement enables users to filter position tiles with more pre-configured options, providing a more consistent experience in searching for positions.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • Include Generic Objects in filters in the Position Tile view is selected in Admin Center>Succession Settings.

5- Unhide Individual Positions or Profiles in the Succession Org Chart:

When you've hidden some positions or profiles from the Succession Org Chart, you can now view their names and choose which one to unhide.

If you unhide one position or profile, you will be directed to that specific node in the org chart. If you unhide multiple ones, you will remain at your current node.

Previously, you could only unhide all of them at once, without knowing exactly what had been unhidden.

Enablement: Automatically on

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Re: Dynamically Reading Blobs from Azure Blob Storage to CPI
entry.content:
Hello @Nic_Frigius , and thank you for this great blog , 

At HTTP adapter step, i am getting an 401 error, 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?><Error><Code>InvalidAuthenticationInfo</Code><Message>Server failed to authenticate the request. Please refer to the information in the www-authenticate header.
RequestId:cfb7fe53-b01e-003d-7281-a54712000000
Time:2025-04-04T16:48:10.0343804Z</Message><AuthenticationErrorDetail>Audience validation failed. Audience did not match.</AuthenticationErrorDetail></Error>

I followed the same steps when I created the client Credential . Do you have any idea please ?

entry.title: Re: How to disable "Post" authorization in app "Change Pa...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community Q&A blog.

To disable the "Post" authorization in the app "Change Parked Journal Entries" (FBV2) in SAP S/4HANA, you need to control this through authorization objects in the user roles.

Here's how to proceed:

 

  1. Understand the Authorization Object Involved

The "Post" function in FBV2 is controlled primarily by:

  • Authorization object: F_BKPF_BUK — Accounting Document: Authorization for Company Codes
  • Authorization object: F_BKPF_KOA — Accounting Document: Authorization for Account Types
  • Authorization object: F_LFA1_BEK — Vendors: Account Authorization

 

But specifically for posting, this object is key:

  • F_TR_BUK – Posting Authorization for Company Code

In addition, for parked document processing:

  • F_FBU_BUK – Authorizations for Parked Documents

 

  1. Adjust Roles in PFCG (Role Maintenance)

To disable posting but still allow changing parked documents in FBV2:

  1. Go to transaction PFCG
  2. Open the user's role that grants access to FBV2
  3. In the Authorizations tab, locate:
    • Object: F_FBU_BUK
  4. In the ACTVT (Activity) field:
    • Make sure only the following are allowed:
      • 02 – Change
      • Optionally: 03 – Display
    • Remove 01 (Create) and especially 05 (Post)
  • Keep 02 for change if you want them to change parked docs
  • Remove 05 to block posting of parked documents

 

  1. Optional: SU24 Check

Run SU24 for FBV2 to check which authorization objects are called by this transaction, and fine-tune based on actual checks.

 

I hope this explanation will help you solve your problem.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Document 4500006700 does not contain any selectable i...
entry.content: Once you have received the consignment stock in the Warehouse or Sloc-PO job is done. Make sure you have a consignment info record maintained and do 411+K without any reference. You can use PO to do 411K.

entry.title: COMING SOON - the place to come to discover our always-free academic content and resources.
entry.content: This knowledge base features content created by University Alliances and lecturers from across our global community.

entry.title: Re: SAC story date selection as static
entry.content: changed to Flat Presentation and selection type : Sigle selection its worked

entry.title: Is SAP Signavio Process Insights compatible with S/4 Hana PUBLIC Cloud?
entry.content: Is SAP Signavio Process Insights compatible with S/4 Hana PUBLIC Cloud?

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content: So we have maxquerytask set to 1:

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Signavio Process Insights compatible with S/4...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community Q&A blog.

Yes, SAP Signavio Process Insights is compatible with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, there are some key considerations to keep in mind :

 

  1. Compatibility & Integration

Native integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public & Private Editions).

Uses SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) as a middleware to connect with S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Pre-built connectors for extracting and analyzing process data from S/4HANA Cloud.

 

  1.  Key Features in Public Cloud Context

Process Mining & Insights: Identifies inefficiencies in business processes.

Standard KPIs & Benchmarks: Uses predefined performance indicators for process analysis.

Improvement Recommendations: Suggests automation and best practices tailored to public cloud constraints.

Limited Customization: Since S/4HANA Public Cloud has restricted extensibility, some deep custom process analyses may not be feasible compared to Private Cloud.

 

  1. Considerations for Public Cloud

Access to process data is managed via SAP BTP event-driven architecture (some configurations may require additional setup).

Real-time data processing may be limited compared to on-premise or private cloud, depending on available APIs.

 

I hope this explanation will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content: SET OPTION PUBLIC.ansi_update_constraints = 'off'; -- different from default 'Cursors' SET OPTION PUBLIC.auditing_options = '2'; -- different from default '4294967295' SET OPTION PUBLIC.continue_after_raiserror = 'OFF'; -- different from default 'On' SET OPTION PUBLIC.login_procedure = 'NFR.login_check'; -- different from default 'sp_login_environment' SET OPTION PUBLIC.max_query_tasks = '1'; -- different from default '0' SET OPTION PUBLIC.max_temp_space = '32G'; -- different from default '0' SET OPTION PUBLIC.min_password_length = '0'; -- different from default '3' SET OPTION PUBLIC.sr_timestamp_format = 'hh:nn:ss.Ssssss yyyy/mm/dd'; -- different from default 'yyyy/mm/dd hh:nn:ss.Ssssss' SET OPTION PUBLIC.string_rtruncation = 'OFF'; -- different from default 'On'

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
Not able to create a user using the RBSC portal using the P user ID?

 

 

Need help please.

 

Thanks 

Vishala

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: But not able to run the project !

entry.title: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to produciton
entry.content:
Customer wants to pick whole pallets and the pallets have several batches on them. When setting rounding to storage type or pocess type it will round to want is need to the next batch and that is all. So if there are the batchs and the second batch satifies the order and it rounds to that batch the last batch on the pallet is not requested.

 

We have changed it to Batch Neutral storage type and now it requests everthing on the pallet. My question is can you still use the FEFO rule for stock removal. Some testing has given different results and just not sure if the data is bad,

@Christian_Ries

entry.title: Re: In CDS view group by clause not aggregating all records
entry.content:
if the value is different, how they can be grouped???

do you understand how group works?

entry.title: Shaping the Future with Data and AI: SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine and Generative AI Toolkit
entry.content:
We’re pleased to announce the general availability of two groundbreaking technologies: the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine and the Generative AI Toolkit. These solutions are designed to elevate your business intelligence and AI capabilities, offering enhanced data connectivity, smarter insights, and faster decision-making.

This blog post highlights the features of these new offerings and showcases how they align with SAP’s long-standing commitment to deliver innovations that drive your business forward. More importantly, it’s our way of saying: We’ve kept our promise.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine: A New Era of Data Connectivity

At SAP TechEd 2024, we introduced a major leap forward in how businesses can manage and navigate their data. The SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine empowers organizations to integrate, query, and leverage their data in entirely new ways by making connections and relationships between data points transparent. The new engine in SAP HANA Cloud enables you to fully harness your data's potential, driving smarter analytics, streamlined data management, and enhanced business results.

The Knowledge Graph Engine unlocks a deeper understanding of your data by capturing and analyzing complex relationships—something traditional databases struggle to do. This feature introduces a semantic layer on top of your data, enabling you to define, connect, and visualize entities across multiple sources. It’s built to handle and optimize data in the triple store format, an efficient method for representing complex, interconnected data, which enables better decision-making and more flexible data models.

Key Features:

  • Informed Decision-Making: Utilizing the Knowledge Graph Engine enables you to extract richer insights from your data, facilitating more accurate and well-informed decisions.
  • SQL and SPARQL Interoperability: The Knowledge Graph Engine will support SPARQL, the specialized query language for knowledge graph data, while tightly integrating it with SQL.
  • Enhanced Data Structure: With RDF and SPARQL's structured framework, your data models gain enhanced logical precision, leading to more dependable and accurate results.
  • Enhanced AI & Analytics: Provides structured context to AI-driven applications, improving decision-making, recommendations, and search accuracy.
  • Automated Reasoning & Inference: Uses built-in logic to infer new relationships from existing data, minimizing manual data maintenance as with the case of relational data models.

Whether you’re looking to enhance your data governance, improve the discoverability of insights, or develop smarter applications, the Knowledge Graph Engine will be a game-changer in how you think about and use data.

 

Generative AI Toolkit: Unlocking Innovation & Productivity

Alongside the Knowledge Graph Engine, we’re also launching the Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud. As the world of AI continues to evolve, our toolkit is designed to make AI more accessible and usable for our customers. The Generative AI Toolkit is a powerful solution designed to accelerate AI-driven development and data analysis. Users can for example, effortlessly query datasets using natural language and receive results through intuitive AI-driven interactions.

Key Features:

  • Open-Source Python Library & Integration: Leverage advanced generative AI capabilities through Python, enabling intuitive development and smooth integration for AI-driven tasks.
  • Natural Language-Driven Analysis & Automated Code Generation: Perform dataframe analysis with natural language queries and automatically generate code based on best practices and business scenarios code templates.
  • Retrieval Augmented Generation & LLMs Integration: Support for RAG with multiple vector stores and integration with large language models (LLMs) via the Generative AI Hub SDK for more accurate and context-aware AI solutions.
  • Unlocking SAP HANA Cloud’s Database AI Engine Capabilities: Utilizing targeted tools to generate machine learning models for scenarios like Time Series Forecasting on presented business data.
  • Streamlined Machine Learning Application Development: Accelerate the infusion of machine learning scenarios, like classification, regression or time series forecasting into CAP applications with agentic, natural language assistance. This allows for a much faster getting-started experience, simplifying the overall embedded AI application development process.

As organizations seek to empower their workforce with smarter, AI-powered tools, the Generative AI Toolkit offers the scalability and flexibility needed to drive innovation, improve decision-making, and accelerate time-to-value across the organization.

 

Keep the Promise

Both the Knowledge Graph Engine and the Generative AI Toolkit were born out of feedback from our customers and years of research into the challenges businesses face in a data-driven world. In our previous announcements and at past SAP TechEd events, we made bold commitments to revolutionize how organizations use data and AI. We’ve delivered on those promises with these two cutting-edge solutions.

In line with our dedication to providing continuous value, we’ve ensured that the Knowledge Graph Engine and Generative AI Toolkit are production-ready, offering you robust, scalable, and secure platforms to build your next-generation applications and analytics.

 

Early Adopter Care Program: Join us on the Journey

As we mark the general availability of these solutions, we also want to extend an invitation to our customers and partners to join our Early Adopter Care (EAC) Programs. These exclusive programs offer personalized support and guidance as you explore and implement the Knowledge Graph Engine or Elasticity in SAP HANA Cloud.

Being part of the EAC programs means you’ll get early access to the latest features, tools, and documentation, as well as direct engagement with SAP experts who can assist you in optimizing the use of these technologies for your specific needs. Additionally, you’ll be able to influence the direction of future updates and enhancements, ensuring that SAP HANA Cloud continues to meet the evolving demands of your business.

How to Get Involved?

Visit the EAC program pages to learn more about how to enroll:

  • Knowledge Graph Engine - EAC Program
  • Elasticity in SAP HANA Cloud - EAC Program

By joining the EAC programs, you will be at the forefront of the next wave of innovation, helping us shape the future of data management and AI.

 

Looking Ahead

I’m incredibly excited about the opportunities these new capabilities bring to the table. From smarter data connectivity to AI-driven productivity, SAP HANA Cloud is setting the stage for a new era of innovation in data and AI.

We believe these advancements will not only help organizations thrive in an increasingly digital world, but also empower individuals across industries to make better, more informed decisions, faster. These technologies will be pivotal in shaping the future of how businesses interact with data, AI, and the world around them.

We’re committed to supporting your digital transformation journey every step of the way, and we can’t wait to see how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve greater success.

Thank you for being part of the SAP community. The journey has only just begun!

entry.title: What configurations/enhancements in SAP EWM we can implement to prioritize warehouse task creation?
entry.content:
Hello SAP EWM Community,

We’re looking to optimize the creation and prioritization of warehouse tasks for high-demand items in our distribution center managed by SAP EWM. Specifically, we aim to reduce the picking time and improve order fulfillment rates for these items.

Key Points:

  1. Our warehouse handles a mix of high- and low-demand items.
  2. Current picking strategies do not adequately prioritize high-demand items, leading to delays.
  3. We utilize standard picking and putaway strategies configured in SAP EWM.

Specific Question:

What configurations or enhancements in SAP EWM can be implemented to prioritize warehouse task creation for high-demand items, ensuring faster picking and improved order fulfillment?

Looking forward to your insights and recommendations!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: EWM - Cancel Picking/ Cancel Outbound Process
entry.content: Nice post!

entry.title: Re: Kanban Replenishment with SAP EWM-One Step Stock Transfer
entry.content:
Nice post !
Thanks.

entry.title: Is there a way we can measure how many times we ran out of stock for a maintenance order
entry.content:
Hi,

We are implementing spare parts inventory indicators and one of those is to measure the stockouts.

In other words, how many times we ran out of stock for a part that was required for a maintenance order at the moment the work was to be performed.

We have tried looking for MSPT status on maintenance orders but that only tells us the order does not currently have the parts needed in inventory but the MRP will run and upon reception of the part, it will remove the MSPT status.

But the order could be due a month after the parts were received.  So this is not a stockout for us.  It is just-in-time supply.

What we want to know is if someone tied to get parts from the store and the parts weren't on the shelf.  I kow we can't capture that in SAP, but I thought maybe there is a way we could detect this situation in some way.

I was looking at MD07 the other day and I'm not entirely sure how we could implement such table in BW, or if anything already exists in BW for that kind of situation.

 

Any ideas anyone?  Or is it hopeless?

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content:
Setting the max_query_tasks option to 1 disables intra-query parallelism. That may degrade performance if queries could be parallelized but prevented from doing so. It is generally best to run with this options default.

Please see my comment to your original question related to MaxMultiprogrammingLevel.

entry.title: Re: Role BUP010 missing when synchronizing Employee to BP in new model
entry.content: @ simonhodge, Can you please help me in details. Why I am not able to find the mentioned OSS note. Please help. I am stuck with this issue.

entry.title: Re: Does the SAP Enable Now Certification will be retired...
entry.content: Will the training content for Enable Now, such as SEN100, continue to be available after April 2025 and if so, until when?

entry.title: Re: Error in Employee as a Business Partner and Synchroni...
entry.content:
@sikindar_a Yes. ABAPer is able to delete the record. Now I am facing another issue

Can you please help me with this. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: 2 vs 4 Physical CPUs
entry.content: Version: 17.0.11.7908, -gna=1, -gn=48, -gnl=48, -gnh=80, current=48

entry.title: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am looking to have the CU50 in internal tables. For several days, I have been trying to debug standard looking for a function or other which will give me what I need but I can't find anything.
I want from my material and the characteristics values and the variant cost :

All components associated with the hierarchy and their amount.

Thanks a lot in advance for your help

BRegards

ABAP Development

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Deep Dive Workshop - HOUSTON TX Edition! April 29th, 2025
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM Deep Dive Workshop - HOUSTON TX Edition! April 29th, 2025

 

We're excited to let you know after the profound success of the SAP ALM Roadshow 2025 Plano TX and SAP ALM SUMMIT NA 2025 @NSQ, we are inviting you to SAP Cloud ALM In-Person Deep Dive Workshop! Houston Texas Edition! April 29th 2025. These events are great opportunities for enterprises to dive into the latest in Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) with SAP's experts, fellow industry leaders, and collaborative representatives from various SAP Lines of Business (Lobs).

 

Event Highlights:

  • Explore the importance of a robust ALM strategy for driving business growth and agility.
  • Gain in-depth knowledge about SAP Cloud ALM and its pivotal role in managing intelligent cloud and hybrid business landscapes.
  • Participate in hands-on sessions for implementation and operations
  • Engage in interactive sessions with SAP ALM experts and network with peers.
  • Discover how to seamlessly transition to SAP Cloud ALM, the go-to platform for all your Application Lifecycle Management needs as an SAP customer.
  • Enhance your expertise by taking the Solution Transformation Consultant Badge test, which will demonstrate your ability to effectively manage the design to deploy phase of business transformations using an integrated approach with an end-to-end tool chain.

 

 

Looking Ahead: The feedback we’ve received Plano TX, SAP ALM Roadshow Event & SAP ALM SUMMIT NA 2025 has been overwhelmingly positive, reinforcing the importance of regional engagements to address specific market needs and we look forward seeing you @ Houston, TX

Conclusion: This immersive full-day event is designed to offer your customers valuable insights and hands-on experience with SAP Cloud ALM Implementation and Operations capabilities, empowering them to drive business transformation and enhance efficiency.

 

Grab your seats below:

Register Now

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud ALM Regional Go To Market, Americas

Email: sapalmus@sap.com
